summary: Reference table is well-researched: all 15 spec paths resolve correctly under cclab/specs/, relevance scores are appropriate, and key requirements are accurate against the actual spec contents (os.md exist_ok/stat gap confirmed, sys.md missing attributes confirmed, json.md signature gaps confirmed, re.md missing flags parameter confirmed, conformance.md R11 xfail list confirmed). Three blockers require revision before spec writing: (1) The spec plan places 13 unrelated stdlib modules in a single api-conformance.md file, violating the one-logical-unit rule — each module spec already exists individually and should be modified, not aggregated. (2) The struct module is one of R11's 12 failing modules (conformance.md line 56: 'struct') but struct-and-binary.md is absent from both the reference table and spec plan. (3) The spec plan sections omit 'requirements', which is mandatory for a conformance-fixing spec — changes without requirements specifies implementation without specifying behavior.

findings.description: The single spec plan entry creates crates/cclab-mamba/stdlib/api-conformance.md to cover conformance gaps across os, sys, json, re, collections, itertools, functools, math, random, datetime, pathlib, io, and hashlib — 13 stdlib modules in one file. This violates the checklist rule 'each spec file covers exactly one logical unit (not multiple unrelated concerns)'. All 13 per-module specs already exist (verified: os.md, sys.md, json.md, re.md, collections.md, itertools.md, functools.md, math.md, random.md, datetime.md, pathlib.md, io.md, hashlib.md all present under cclab/specs/crates/cclab-mamba/stdlib/). The correct design is to add separate spec plan entries with action: modify for each module spec, adding requirement sections for the CPython 3.12 gaps documented in the reference table. Creating a parallel aggregation spec introduces maintenance confusion and duplication with the existing specs.

findings.location: reference_context.md — struct module absent from reference table and spec plan
findings.description: conformance.md R11 (verified at cclab/specs/crates/cclab-mamba/testing/conformance.md line 56) explicitly lists struct as one of the 12 failing stdlib modules: 'For each of the 12 stdlib modules (collections, csv, datetime, functools, hashlib, io, itertools, json, math, random, re, struct)'. The conformance test fixture list confirms this at lines 262, 445-448 (stdlib/struct/struct_conformance.py, struct_conformance.py). The spec crates/cclab-mamba/stdlib/struct-and-binary.md exists (verified) and covers struct.pack, struct.unpack, struct.calcsize, struct.error, struct.unpack_from. This spec must be added to the reference table with high relevance — the key requirements gap is: struct-and-binary.md only specifies basic pack/unpack but misses struct.iter_unpack() (Python 3.4+, standard in 3.12), struct.Struct class (compiled format strings, same-as-compile pattern), and the 'n' and 'N' format characters (ssize_t, size_t) added for platform-native sizes.

The spec plan entry lists sections: [overview, changes, test-plan]. For a conformance-fixing change where the task is to 'audit stdlib module function signatures and return types against CPython 3.12 and fix mismatches' (requirements.md), a requirements section is mandatory. Without it, the spec defines what files to change (changes) and how to test (test-plan) but not WHAT behavior must be true after the change. Each conformance gap in the reference table (e.g., os.listdir path must accept str/bytes/path-like, re module functions must accept flags=0 parameter, json.JSONDecodeError must be a subclass of ValueError with .msg/.doc/.pos/.lineno/.colno) needs a formal requirement ID so implementation can be verified. The requirements section must enumerate these per-function conformance obligations.

findings.location: spec_plan — action: create instead of modify for existing per-module specs
findings.description: The spec plan proposes to create a NEW aggregated spec api-conformance.md rather than modifying the 13 existing per-module specs. Each existing spec (os.md, sys.md, json.md, etc.) already defines the current API surface. The correct spec plan pattern for an audit-and-fix change is to use action: modify against each existing spec to add missing requirement entries. For example: modify os.md adding R6 (os.getpid), R7 (os.getuid/os.access/os.chmod/os.symlink/os.readlink/os.scandir), R8 (os.path.realpath/relpath/expanduser/expandvars); modify sys.md adding R5 (sys.byteorder/executable/version_info/float_info), R6 (sys.getrecursionlimit/setrecursionlimit/getrefcount/getsizeof/intern); etc. This is both spec-structurally cleaner and enables partial merge as individual modules are fixed.

findings.location: reference_context.md — io.md key requirements mix io, csv, and pprint gaps without attribution
findings.description: The io.md key requirements entry lists csv.DictReader, csv.DictWriter, csv.QUOTE_ALL/MINIMAL/NONNUMERIC/NONE alongside io-specific gaps (StringIO.readline/readlines/truncate, BytesIO.read1, io.open, io.DEFAULT_BUFFER_SIZE) and pprint gaps (pformat depth parameter). This is acceptable because io.md is explicitly titled 'io, csv, pprint' (verified: spec header line 5 title is 'stdlib: io, csv, pprint'; R3/R4 cover csv.reader/writer). However, the key requirements text should clarify that csv.DictReader/DictWriter are absent from io.md R3/R4 (which only cover reader/writer, not the Dict variants), making the gap clear: the spec has R3 for csv.reader and R4 for csv.writer but neither covers DictReader/DictWriter which are separate classes in CPython 3.12.

All 15 spec paths in the Spec column are valid relative paths under cclab/specs/ and resolve to existing files. Verified: crates/cclab-mamba/stdlib/os.md → cclab/specs/crates/cclab-mamba/stdlib/os.md ✓, sys.md ✓, json.md ✓, re.md ✓, collections.md ✓, itertools.md ✓, functools.md ✓, math.md ✓, random.md ✓, datetime.md ✓, pathlib.md ✓, io.md ✓, hashlib.md ✓, native-implementations.md ✓; testing/conformance.md ✓, testing/mamba-py312-conformance.md ✓, testing/test-harness.md ✓; mamba-p1-lang-features-spec.md ✓.

findings.location: reference_context.md — key requirements accuracy for os.md
findings.description: All 4 os.md gap claims verified against the actual spec. exist_ok claim: os.md R1 (line 38) says 'must not error if directory already exists (equivalent to exist_ok=True behavior)' — spec bakes in the behavior without exposing the parameter. ✓ listdir gap: os.md R1 shows listdir(path='.') with only str path type; the spec notes 'excludes . and ..' (confirmed line 37). ✓ stat return: os.md R2 says 'Return file metadata (size, mtime)' — no st_mode, st_ino, st_uid, etc. ✓ environ: R4 lists __getitem__, __setitem__, get, keys — missing __contains__, __delitem__, items, values, pop. ✓

findings.location: reference_context.md — key requirements accuracy for sys.md
findings.description: sys.md attribute and function gaps verified. The spec Attributes table has: argv, path, version, platform, maxsize, modules, stdout, stderr, stdin (9 attributes). Missing from spec: byteorder, builtin_module_names, executable, flags, float_info, int_info, version_info — all correctly identified as absent. Functions table has only sys.exit(code=0) — getrecursionlimit, setrecursionlimit, getrefcount, getsizeof, intern all correctly identified as missing. sys.version 'Mamba version string' claim confirmed at R4.

findings.location: reference_context.md — key requirements accuracy for json.md
findings.description: json.md gaps verified. Functions table shows json.dumps(obj, indent=None) and json.loads(s) — sort_keys, ensure_ascii, separators, default, cls, object_hook, parse_float, parse_int all absent from spec. ✓ json.JSONDecodeError claim: R2 says 'raise json.JSONDecodeError (or ValueError)' — the (or ValueError) weakens the type contract; CPython requires json.JSONDecodeError specifically as a subclass of ValueError. ✓ Dict key coercion: R1 'Dict keys must be strings; non-string keys raise TypeError' — correctly identifies that CPython actually coerces int/float/bool/None keys by default. ✓ json.load()/json.dump() absent from Functions table. ✓

findings.location: reference_context.md — key requirements accuracy for re.md
findings.description: re.md gaps verified. Module-level functions table (search, match, fullmatch, findall, finditer, sub, subn, split, escape, compile) — none have a flags parameter shown. ✓ Match object table shows group/groups/start/end/span — .string, .re, .pos, .endpos, .lastindex, .lastgroup absent. ✓ re.sub count: R3 correctly mentions count parameter. ✓ re.IGNORECASE, re.MULTILINE, re.DOTALL, re.VERBOSE constants absent from spec. ✓ Compiled pattern attributes (.pattern, .flags, .groups, .groupindex) absent from R5. ✓

findings.location: reference_context.md — native-implementations.md and conformance.md relevance and accuracy
findings.description: native-implementations.md (high) correctly identifies serde_json, regex crate, chrono, rusqlite as backends; error mapping layer (R4 confirmed); registration flow via register_stdlib()->register()->module_registry (confirmed in spec sequence diagram); stubs vs implementations distinction (confirmed in spec Overview: 'stubs or very basic manual implementations'). conformance.md (high) correctly cites R11 with 18 xfail fixture files, flat+nested paths, regen_golden.py regeneration strategy. Both accuracy ratings pass.

All relevance scores are appropriate. The 13 direct stdlib module specs are high (they directly define what must change). native-implementations.md is high (defines the Rust backend that must be updated). conformance.md is high (defines the test strategy and R11 requirements). mamba-py312-conformance.md is medium (complementary P2 work sharing the same test harness). test-harness.md is medium (infrastructure shared by conformance tests). mamba-p1-lang-features-spec.md is low (background, bytes type from R5 affects hashlib/re/io interop). All scores are defensible.

revisions_required: Split the single stdlib-api-conformance-high-priority spec plan entry into 13 separate modify entries, one per existing per-module spec (os.md, sys.md, json.md, re.md, collections.md, itertools.md, functools.md, math.md, random.md, datetime.md, pathlib.md, io.md, hashlib.md). Each entry should have action: modify and sections: [requirements, changes] — adding the specific CPython 3.12 gap requirements identified in the reference table key requirements column.

Add crates/cclab-mamba/stdlib/struct-and-binary.md to the reference table with high relevance. Key requirements: struct-and-binary.md R1/R2 cover basic pack/unpack/calcsize but miss struct.iter_unpack(fmt, buffer) (Python 3.12 API), the struct.Struct compiled class (Struct(fmt).pack(*v), Struct(fmt).unpack(b)), and format characters 'n'/'N' (ssize_t/size_t). Add a corresponding modify entry to the spec plan with sections: [requirements, changes].

Add 'requirements' to the sections list in every spec plan entry. For a conformance-fixing change, each modified spec needs formal requirement IDs enumerating the CPython 3.12 obligations (e.g., for sys.md: R5: sys.byteorder must be 'little' or 'big'; R6: sys.getrecursionlimit() must return current recursion limit as int). Without requirement IDs the implementation has no verifiable acceptance criteria.
